programmes.show_description: How do they turn Sheffield steel into a carpenter's handsaw, make a microphone that can pick up a whisper, and safely steer through a frozen river?
How do they brew moonshine in the heart of New York City, make a battery powered bicycle, and grow the rubber for wetsuits in the Arizona desert?
BMW M5. Mike finds a classic BMW M5 for the bargain price of £4000. But will Edd be able to fix up this unloved car with its groaning suspension and rattling alternators?
Renault Alpine. Mike is in France to hunt down the rare Renault Alpine 310. The car he finds is great, but it has some surprises in store, and Edd is charged with getting it fixed.
Sam and Henry look through an eclectic collection in Hertfordshire. There's lots for the guys to admire, including a Humber Sceptre and a model aircraft.
Henry and Sam are in Oxfordshire to rummage through a collection of vintage tractors and automobiles. They find a Suzuki SB200 and paraffin dispenser.
Discover how they manufacture false teeth, produce steel safes, build aeroplanes and create maple syrup.
Discover how they create fish farms, produce jellied sweets, manufacture aluminium cans and craft bronze sculptures.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Dominic is challenged to repair a traditional tabletop game that has kept three generations of one family entertained. And, Steve works on a wooden duck.
Preserving British History. Drew Pritchard and Viki Knott search for items that preserve Britain's history. They visit a brass foundry, a stately home, and a salvage yard.
Buy What You Like. Drew Pritchard and Alister Dryburgh uncover hidden gems, including a collection of vintage motorbikes and a new antiques shop in Devon.
A Secretive Box. Angus clears a home of classic books to fund a trip for a widower, before the discovery of an unusual violin hits the high notes at auction.
Send In The Cavalry! From a D-Day hero's eccentric trove to church minister's heartfelt downsizing, Angus sells a huge hippo and some holy bling.
A trip through Scotland sees Natasha get to grips with haggis whilst Steven falls for an exquisite French lady. Who will take crown at the final auction?
A Tonne Of Bricks. Angus helps an army veteran shift a mountain of Lego to raise money for his daughter, and a trip to London unearths exquisite Victorian jewellery.
Fond Farewells & Wedding Bells. Angus clears a home full of memories to help a widow start a new chapter. A couple needs Angus's help to raise some cash to pay for their wedding.
Helmets And Heirlooms. Angus helps a widower sell nautical treasures, including a diving helmet. He also visits a family clearing their late gran's home, uncovering gold.
Railway Relics & A Harley Hog. Angus steams into a train treasure trove while helping a bereaved daughter, then hopes to turn a campervan dream into reality with a Harley sale.
Susannah Constantine & Leslie Ash. Angus raids TV presenter Susannah's couture stash, then helps actress Leslie uncover treasures, memories, and a few famous surprises!
Angus helps the ex-EastEnders star Samantha Womack shift her showbiz things, and former MP Alan Johnson parts with a chess set and much-loved guitar.
Christina Trevanion and Mark Stacey carry on the penultimate leg of their journey in the West Midlands. They pick up antiques on the way to Stockport.
Christina Trevanion and Mark Stacey begin the final stretch of their trip. They look for treasures in a last-ditch effort as they head to Bolton.
Henry Cole and the team take on a classic 1950s Massey Ferguson combine harvester in one of their biggest restorations yet.
Lucia revives a painting with a touching history. Plus, Maria hopes stain glass expert Matt can fix her guilty conscious as well as her broken window.
Eloise brings in what could be the trickiest restoration yet for ceramics expert Kirsten. Meanwhile, a beloved vintage piano stool gets a new lease of life.
How do they mine one of the rarest gemstones in the world, craft the classic cowboy boot, and build a road sweeper that can pick up seven tonnes of trash?
How do they make the world's softest duvets, drill for oil thousands of metres below ground, and put the stretch in rubber bands?
How do they build a diesel engine that can fly, make cough sweets, turn clear plastic into bank notes, and whip up ice cream flavours?
How do they turn a family car into a fortress on wheels, grow millions of perfect Christmas trees and hammer out the world's finest cymbals?
Porsche 914. Mike finds a 914 in a barn, but under the muck he can see a profit in the 70's sports car, so he buys it for a bargain price. Can Edd restore it to a saleable condition?
G Wagon. Mike wants a G Wagen - the popular German four-by-four. He finds one and Edd gives it a facelift, with a 2012 front end and rear lights. Will they land a big profit?
In Devon, Henry and Sam are on a mission to buy automobilia for a client's man cave. The guys uncover a 1937 petrol pump and a pricey tyre inflator.
After eight years of waiting, the guys are finally granted access to an incredible collection. They immediately spot a rare 1930 Wolseley Messenger.
Discover how they manufacture artificial limbs, make peanut butter and produce high intensity light bulbs.
Discover how they manufacture people carriers, build shopping trolleys and produce collectable coins.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Getting Hot. Wayne's on a hunt for a Volvo 960 station wagon. However, the original owner, Paul Newman, fitted this car with a supercharged 5.0 litre V8 engine from a Ford Mustang.
The Power Of Three. After finding a 1970 Porsche 911 E Targa with 5,000 miles, Wayne's got his hands on possibly the world's most perfect 1962 Jaguar Mark 2 3.8 Saloon.
The Three Bills. Wayne's starting off the auction season in Amelia Island where he'll be showing a rare Renault Jolly and also helping to sell a 1988 Porsche 959 Sport.
Porsche 914. Mike finds a 914 in a barn, but under the muck he can see a profit in the 70's sports car, so he buys it for a bargain price. Can Edd restore it to a saleable condition?
Porsche 997. Mike nabs a Porsche 997 at a bargain price of just £15,000. The catch? It's got an engine with a serious case of bore score. Can Elvis fire it up?
Porsche 944 Turbo - Part 1. Mike and Edd are on cloud nine when they get their hands on a Porsche. But it's not all smooth sailing for the boys on this turbo-charged restoration.
Porsche 944 Turbo - Part 2. The mechanical maestros continue to work on the spectacular Porsche Turbo, using all their skills to make sure this is an unforgettable overhaul.
Porsche 993 Targa. Mike goes in search of what many consider to be the last truly great Porsche, and he finds the cheapest 993 in the country. Can the duo get it back to its best?
Porsche 911. Mike and Elvis take on a Porsche 911 in rough shape: it turns up in pieces on a trailer. If they can revive it, they'll take it on an epic drive through the Cairngorms.
1976 Porsche 912E. Mike seeks out a Porsche 912E. With the help of Edd, they address the years of neglect, while they bring home the horses and return on their investment.
1977 Porsche 924. Mike takes on a Porsche 924, the first car he ever turned around on the series. Will fixing oil leaks and worn interior be enough earn a profit?
Morris Minor. The boys visit Fuzz's friend Alex and his Rover collection, but they're sidetracked by a slightly troubled British icon: the Morris Minor Splittie.
Hot Rod. Henry and Fuzz snap up a vintage Oldsmobile hot rod with a legendary V8. Can they get it race-ready for a showdown at Turweston?
Trabant. Henry and Fuzz head to Kent to visit Martin's eclectic collection. There's a classic Jaguar XJ, but a Cold War-era VEB Trabant steals their attention.
Firebird. Henry and Fuzz visit Danny in Basingstoke to check out his massive collection. They're tempted by a 2CV van but are stunned by an impressive Pontiac.
Heading In The Right Direction. The Cogs take centre stage at Britain's premiere car show, but the wheels fall off when Richard returns James May's restored Triumph Stag.
Rio Whistler. Henry and Fuzz visit James in Burnham-on-Crouch to revive his rare Rio Whistler military vehicle. Can they return this weathered veteran to duty?
The teddy bear team bring a giant toy lion back to his glory. Optics expert Richard brings a much-loved harbourmaster's telescope back into focus.
Rock Relics & Robbery Files. Irita discovers a stash of Great Train Robbery papers. Plus, she sells a grandad's 6000 comics and rare coins to fund his baby granddaughter's future.
A flood forces the Desert Diggers to call for help. Plus, new technology leads Jacqui and Andrew to a massive haul of gold.
